parentQuestion: A 45-year-old presents with 'the worst headache of my life,' sudden onset during...

scaffolds.q: Cerebral aneurysms are balloon-like dilations in brain arteries.
scaffolds.explain: Cerebral aneurysms (berry aneurysms) are saccular dilations at arterial bifurcations, typically in the Circle of Willis. They're usually asymptomatic until they rupture, causing subarachnoid hemorrhage. Invite this in:the anatomy — weakness at branch points becomes disaster.

scaffolds.q: What is the classic description of subarachnoid hemorrhage headache?
scaffolds.explain: 'Thunderclap headache' — sudden onset, maximal intensity within seconds, described as the worst headache of the patient's life. This is subarachnoid hemorrhage until proven otherwise. Track this carefully:: the descriptive language patients use tells you the pathology.
